data.title: Nikkei Slips 0.3%, Dragged by Auto, Retail Stocks — Market Talk
data.description: Japanese stocks are lower as uncertainty over the U.S. tariff impact on earnings persists. Auto and retail shares are leading declines. Honda Motor is down 1.7% and Aeon Co. is 1.8% lower. USD/JPY is at 146.83, compared with 147.17 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on any developments related to the potential leadership election for the ruling Liberal Democratic Party of Japan. The country's industrial output fell in July, reflecting the impact of higher U.S. duties, government data showed earlier Friday. The Nikkei Stock Average is down 0.3% at 42707.78. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

data.title: Australia's Harvey Norman hits record high on FY profit beat, 'strong' start to FY26
data.description:
** Harvey Norman soars as much as 14.7% to a record high of A$7.090; marks biggest intraday pct jump since August 2009

** Stock among top 10 performers on ASX200 index

** Furniture and electronics retailer posts FY25 net income of A$518 mln ($336.6 mln), up 47% y/y and above Visible Alpha consensus of A$473.8 mln

** Aggregated sales in July rises 9.9% vs PCP; comparable sales up 8.7%, signalling strong start to FY26

** Declares final dividend of 14.5 AU cents/share, higher than VA consensus of 12.9 AU cents

** HVN last up 13%, takes YTD gains to 47.11%

($1 = A$1.5389)

data.title: Skies Should Soon Clear for Air New Zealand — Market Talk
data.description: Air New Zealand's challenges are likely to extend through December, but Forsyth Barr finds reasons to be optimistic about the medium-term outlook. Analyst Andy Bowley says Air New Zealand will navigate the worst of its engine maintenance crisis in 1H FY 2026. Beyond then, however, things should brighten. The engine issue will gradually improve and be fully resolved by 2H FY 2028, Forsyth Barr says. Air New Zealand will reap more of its transformation benefits. Domestic demand should begin a cyclical recovery, and Air New Zealand's so-called "premiumization strategy" should bring incremental opportunities to lift revenue, the bank says. Still, Forsyth Barr stays at neutral, noting Air New Zealand's return on capital will stay suppressed even as earnings improve over the next 3-4 years. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: US small caps quietly notch historic outperformance vs tech: McGeever
data.description:
Amid the Federal Reserve drama and deluge of corporate earnings in August, one clear but overlooked trend emerged in U.S. equities: the rotation out of expensive tech stocks and into cheaper small caps. As the month draws to a close, the big question is whether this can continue.

The Nasdaq 100 is currently on track for a monthly gain of 1.5% while the Russell 2000 small cap index is headed for a 7.3% rise, signaling an underperformance of 580 basis points for the tech-heavy index.

According to Stuart Kaiser, head of equity trading strategy at Citi, that relative monthly performance for the Nasdaq 100 is in the bottom 5% since 1985.

And if we look at ETFs, tech's underperformance looks even more striking. This month, the Invesco QQQ exchange-traded fund tracking the Nasdaq 100 is flat, while the iShares Russell 2000 ETF is up 7%.

FED BOOST

So what's responsible for this dramatic divergence?

It may partly just reflect investors seeking to rebalance their concentrated and lopsided portfolios. But the split was clearly turbo-charged by Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's Jackson Hole speech on August 22, when he opened the door to an interest rate cut next month.

Manish Kabra, head of U.S. equity strategy at Societe Generale, says the Russell 2000 index's outperformance against the broader S&P 500 that day was the biggest since the U.S. election on November 6 last year that returned Donald Trump to the White House.

Powell's dovish pivot is helping small caps outperform because these companies tend to benefit more from lower interest rates given that they rely on borrowing to grow and expand. Larger firms, especially 'Big Tech' megacaps, often have huge cash reserves and easier access to other sources of financing.

To be sure, lower rates wouldn't just be good news for small caps. The rising tide of liquidity and investor sentiment would typically be expected to lift all boats, including the 'Magnificent Seven' megaships.

As analysts at UBS point out, past equity bubbles have often been burst by rising interest rates, so a resumption of the central bank's easing cycle would appear to minimize that particular risk for high-priced tech stocks.

But, regardless, small caps may still continue to get more of a Fed boost in the near term.

AI DOUBTS

Another catalyst for the rotation has been creeping doubts about AI's ability to deliver returns commensurate with the bubble-like frenzy surrounding the new technology.

Tech stocks remain on the pricey side. That's justified, argue UBS analysts, by the potential revenue from AI-generated efficiencies, which they estimate could reach around $1.5 trillion a year globally.

Others are less optimistic. If value creation on this massive scale fails to materialize, then tech companies will struggle to generate a return on the trillions of dollars of global capex expected in the coming years.

That's why all eyes were on $4.4 trillion Nvidia's earnings on Wednesday. How investors interpret the global AI leader's results will help determine whether tech's underperformance continues into September. The company's revenue, profit and forecasts looked solid, but uncertainty surrounding the suspension of its business with China and skepticism around the revenue outlook are giving investors pause.

UNDER THE RADAR

For now, market momentum remains with smaller firms. Francis Gannon, co-CIO and managing director at Royce Investment Partners, calls it a "stealth summer" for small caps, the recent outperformance of which has gone "mostly unnoticed" amid the daily headlines centered on economic uncertainty, geopolitical worries, and new highs in the larger cap-led indices.

Indeed, the Russell 2000 has yet to revisit last November's peak, while the Nasdaq and S&P 500 have been printing new highs for weeks.

Whether or not small caps start hitting new records will likely be determined by what happens at the Fed.

So the big macroeconomic events to watch next month will be the August employment report due on September 5, August CPI inflation data on September 11, and then, of course, the Fed's policy decision on September 17.

Small caps have enjoyed a pleasant end to the summer. Let's see what happens when investors all get back to their desks next month.

(The opinions expressed here are those of the author, a columnist for Reuters)

data.title: India's economy likely slowed in April-June ahead of US tariff blow
data.description:
Key points:
  • Economy likely expanded 6.7% in April-June, Reuters poll finds
  • Good rainfall, easing inflation likely supported domestic demand
  • US tariffs pose threat to textiles, footwear, chemical sectors
  • Economists say US tariffs may cut growth by 60-80 bps over a year
  • GDP data to be released on Friday at 1030 GMT

India’s economy likely slowed in the April-June quarter as weak urban demand and slow private investment weighed on growth, with U.S. tariff hikes expected to hurt key exports including textiles, footwear, chemicals and food items in the quarters ahead.

The United States on Wednesday doubled its tariffs on Indian goods to as high as 50%, citing New Delhi’s buying of Russian oil - the most punishing rate among U.S. trading partners on a par with Brazil, in a move economists say could hurt growth and jobs.

Gross domestic product (INGDPQ=ECI) is expected to have grown 6.7% year-on-year in April-June, down from 7.4% in the previous three months, according to a Reuters poll of economists.

Even at this pace, India remains one of the fastest-growing major economies. The central bank expects full-year growth to remain close to 6.5% and sees "minimal impact" from higher tariffs, its governor Sanjay Malhotra has said.

The Ministry of Statistics will release GDP data for April-June, the first quarter of fiscal 2025/26, on Friday at 1030 GMT.

Economists say a good monsoon, strong government spending, easing food inflation and front-loaded U.S. shipments likely supported growth in the quarter despite weak urban demand and slow private investment.

Even as real GDP growth holds up, nominal GDP growth, which includes the impact of inflation, is expected to have softened to 8% after averaging almost 11% over the previous eight quarters, according to JPMorgan.

Retail inflation eased to 1.55% in July, an eight-year low.

Lower nominal growth, driven by multi-year low inflation, is likely to have weighed on government tax revenues and corporate profits.

Annual sales growth of 1,736 listed private manufacturing firms eased to 5.3% in the June quarter, from 6.6% in the previous quarter, a report from the Reserve Bank of India, the central bank, showed earlier this week.

Some economists fear prolonged higher U.S. tariffs could dent India's growth further in the coming quarters as exports slow and limit the country's appeal as an alternative manufacturing hub to China.

"If it sticks for a year, GDP growth can slide by 0.7 percentage point, with much of the burden falling on labour-intensive sectors such as jewellery, textiles, and food items," Pranjul Bhandari, chief economist at HSBC, said in a note on Thursday.

The government has pledged support for sectors hit by Washington's tariffs and planned tax cuts to boost domestic consumption.

In its monthly economic report on Wednesday, the finance ministry said planned goods and services tax cuts on essentials would ease household costs and lift demand, while S&P Global's recent ratings upgrade could lower borrowing costs, attract foreign capital and support growth.

data.title: Hallenstein Glasson Price Target Raised 6.4% to NZ$10.80/Share by Forsyth Barr
Air New Zealand Price Target Cut 7.1% to NZ$0.65/Share by Forsyth Barr
Plenty to Be Curious About in Cettire's Annual Result — Market Talk
data.description: There's plenty in Cettire's annual result to arouse RBC Capital Markets' curiosity. Analyst Wei-Weng Chen says the departure of Daniel Agostinelli from Cettire's board after just four months appears unusual. "As does the decision to give material cash pay rises to the CEO and CFO in a time of stress," RBC says. For RBC, Cettire's balance sheet is the key issue. Despite only a modest Ebitda loss in June, cash fell an additional A$8 million that month. "Adding to our balance sheet concerns is a reclassification of A$22 million of VAT receivables to non-current with Cettire noting that while the receivables are recoverable, timing is uncertain," RBC says. It has an underperform call on Cettire, which is up 17% at A$0.35 today. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Summerset Price Target Cut 3.3% to NZ$11.70/Share by Forsyth Barr
Nikkei Slips 0.3%, Dragged by Auto, Retail Stocks — Market Talk
data.description:
2019 ET - Japanese stocks are lower as uncertainty over the U.S. tariff impact on earnings persists. Auto and retail shares are leading declines. Honda Motor is down 1.7% and Aeon Co. is 1.8% lower. USD/JPY is at 146.83, compared with 147.17 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on any developments related to the potential leadership election for the ruling Liberal Democratic Party of Japan. The country's industrial output fell in July, reflecting the impact of higher U.S. duties, government data showed earlier Friday. The Nikkei Stock Average is down 0.3% at 42707.78. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

1948 ET - Japanese stocks may trade rangebound as uncertainty over the U.S. tariff impact on earnings continues. Nikkei futures are flat at 42920 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 146.87, compared with 147.17 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on Japanese economic data, including industrial production due later, as well as domestic politics. Consumer prices, excluding volatile fresh food, rose 2.5% from a year earlier for August in Tokyo, matching a 2.5% rise expected, government data showed earlier Friday. The Nikkei Stock Average rose 0.7% to 42828.79 on Thursday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

1905 ET - Fed governor Christopher Waller, the first Fed governor to speak publicly since President Trump moved to fire Lisa Cook on Monday, declines to comment on her situation. "Things are now in the hands of lawyers and the courts," Waller says, answering a question about the situation from a moderator after a speech delivered in Miami on Thursday evening. Earlier Thursday, Cook filed a lawsuit in federal court to keep her job. (matt.grossman@wsj.com; @mattgrossman)

1848 ET - Jefferies is a big fan of everything about car dealership Eargers Automotive--except its valuation. Eagers's share price rose 12% on Thursday after beating expectations in 1H. Revenue of A$6.5 billion was 7% ahead of consensus hopes, while gross profit was 2% ahead of estimates. Jefferies says Eagers controls 14% of new vehicle sales in Australia, compared to the largest U.S. dealer, which has 2% of the market there. It's also big in used-vehicle sales. That makes it attractive for auto makers looking to establish a retail presence in Australia. Still, Jefferies says Eagers's valuation metrics following Thursday's short squeeze just look too high. "The market appears to be pricing in deals yet to happen," says analyst John Campbell. "We can't get there." (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1546 ET - Treasury yields are mixed ahead of July PCE inflation and as the battle for control of the Fed escalates. Governor Cook sues Trump to block his move to fire her, which has put the institution's independence in question. Some economists say the threat from the administration could be an upward driver for long-term Treasury yields. Tomorrow, headline PCE inflation is expected to slow down and core to accelerate, both slightly, in a WSJ survey. The 10-year declines 0.031 percentage point to 4.206% and the two-year rises 0.010 p.p. to 3.634%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1444 ET - Kohl's second-quarter results are further evidence the department-store chain is losing share to other retail channels, including off-price retailers, UBS analysts say in a note. While the results were better than expected, Kohl's same-store sales declined for the fifteenth consecutive quarter, and with the company in the early stages of a turnaround plan, there likely won't be any meaningful improvement until at least 2026, the analysts say. They expect Wall Street's earnings per-share estimates to move lower over the next year given weak sales, tariff-related cost increases and an increase in promotional offers during the coming holiday season compared to the prior year, keeping sentiment muted. Shares fall 6.1% to $15.19. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1416 ET - Jack Daniel's Tennessee Whiskey maker Brown-Forman sees weakness in the U.S. market for spirits as slightly more cyclical than structural, CEO Lawson Whiting says on a call with analysts. Inflation and high interest rates are still hurting customers' ability to spend, and tariffs are putting pressure on them as well, Whiting says. Structural considerations-like the rising popularity of cannabis, more use of GLP-1 drugs and a general wellness push among consumers--wouldn't be able to tank the alcohol market as quickly as has been seen, the CEO says. "There is some continuum between cyclical and structural," he says. "But I still feel like the cyclical side is a little bit bigger piece of it." Shares retreat 5.1% to $28.89. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1211 ET - Gold futures rise on mounting bets of a September U.S. interest-rate cut. Futures are up 0.55% at $3,467.70 a troy ounce. The precious metal has made steady gains since Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's speech last Friday, in which he opened the door to easing monetary policy, Trade Nation's David Morrison says in a note. Investors are also keeping a close eye on the weaker U.S. dollar, which is currently influencing gold prices to an extent, Morrison says. A weaker dollar is less competitive with gold's safe-haven aspects and makes it cheaper for international investors to buy dollar-denominated bullion. In regards to the dollar, upcoming data releases including core personal consumption expenditure data on Friday should keep the market on alert, Morrison says. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

1140 ET - U.S. activity and labor indicators do little to change the prospect of an interest rate cut by the Fed next month, and Treasury yields weaken with some curve flattening. GDP in the 2Q expands by an annualized rate of 3.3%, while jobless claims decline a little. Economists expect both output growth and job creation to slow in coming months. August payrolls are due next week, with some forecasters calling for an uptick in unemployment. The 10-year Treasury yield is at 4.216%, after settling yesterday at 4.238%. The two-year rises to 3.637% from 3.624%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1112 ET - Credit spreads between French bank bonds and Italian bank bonds narrow as Italian bonds perform better than their French equivalents, ING's Maureen Schuller says in a note. "Italian bank bonds have fared well by the reduced sovereign concerns and related positive rating momentum," she says. Credit spreads between the two asset classes are likely to widen once political stability is restored in France, Schuller says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

1055 ET - The potential collapse of the French government is unlikely to prove a sustained driver of the euro for the remainder of the year, Deutsche Bank's George Saravelos says in a note. "The big picture this year is that despite all the political noise, France has had a de facto coalition government that not only managed to reach agreement on a very tough 2025 budget but also is actually delivering on the tightening that was agreed." Unless the news flow shifts to suggest a far more adverse fiscal outcome, French politics are unlikely to have major impact on the euro-dollar exchange rate in coming months, he says. The euro rises 0.3% to $1.1672. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

1043 ET - The cost of celebrating a friend's big day now rivals the price of keeping a roof over your head, Zillow says. A new Zillow analysis finds that attending just one wedding and a bachelor or bachelorette weekend can set you back about $2,010, comparable to the typical U.S. monthly rent of $2,072. In fact, celebrating a single wedding costs more than a month of rent in nearly 70% of the largest U.S. metros. Nearly half of Gen Z and millennials--45%--say they've made a housing sacrifice to afford these celebrations. The most common trade-offs include renting or buying a smaller home and living with roommates. One-quarter say they've declined to attend at least one wedding event because of the cost. The median age of renters who moved in the past year is 33, nearly identical to the U.S. median age of marriage of 32. (chris.wack@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

2019 ET - Japanese stocks are lower as uncertainty over the U.S. tariff impact on earnings persists. Auto and retail shares are leading declines. Honda Motor is down 1.7% and Aeon Co. is 1.8% lower. USD/JPY is at 146.83, compared with 147.17 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on any developments related to the potential leadership election for the ruling Liberal Democratic Party of Japan. The country's industrial output fell in July, reflecting the impact of higher U.S. duties, government data showed earlier Friday. The Nikkei Stock Average is down 0.3% at 42707.78. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

2014 ET - Car parts company Bapcor's annual result does little to change Jefferies's view that it is a turnaround story. The result was poor overall, Jefferies says. Ebitda from its Trade segment in FY 2025 was up 5% on a year ago, but missed expectations by 3%. Retail earnings were also weaker than hoped. Analyst John Campbell says Bapcor's outlook commentary is very vague and suggests any material improvement is unlikely to be forthcoming in FY 2026. At best, it would seem any meaningful upturn from Bapcor isn't likely before 2H of FY 2027 at the earliest, Jefferies says. Bapcor is down 1.3% at A$3.89 in early trading. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1948 ET - Japanese stocks may trade rangebound as uncertainty over the U.S. tariff impact on earnings continues. Nikkei futures are flat at 42920 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 146.87, compared with 147.17 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on Japanese economic data, including industrial production due later, as well as domestic politics. Consumer prices, excluding volatile fresh food, rose 2.5% from a year earlier for August in Tokyo, matching a 2.5% rise expected, government data showed earlier Friday. The Nikkei Stock Average rose 0.7% to 42828.79 on Thursday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

1944 ET - Australian shares are poised to open lower, with Australian stock futures down by 0.3% before the bell. That is despite the S&P 500 and the Dow industrials notching new closing highs after a government report revealed surprising economic strength. Ahead of the open, Star Entertainment Group said recent trading has stabilized but cautioned that conditions remain soft. Miner BHP priced a 1.4 billion euro bond offer, while uranium company Boss Energy reported an annual loss for fiscal 2025. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

1934 ET - Beacon Lighting's sales are set to accelerate, according to Citi. More green shoots are visible in the Australian housing market, analyst Sam Teeger says. They include less vendor discounting, improved house price growth in July, and clearance rates exceeding historical averages in recent weeks. This activity is being driven by falling interest rates. Australia's central bank has cut rates three times this year and Citi anticipates an additional two reductions by the end of December. Together, this should be positive for Beacon's sales. "Furthermore, Citi's bullish outlook on the consumer from higher spending capacity should also support sales in FY 2026," the bank says. "We forecast like-for-like sales growth of 3% in FY 2026, which is an acceleration from 1.5% in FY 2025." (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1910 ET [Dow Jones]--Wesfarmers's key retail businesses have more sales momentum than Citi expected, prompting the bank to upgrade its stock to neutral. Wesfarmers's Bunnings home-improvements chain is benefiting from an expanded tool shop, which Citi expects will be a meaningful driver of sales growth in FY 2026. "We now expect Bunnings to generate Ebit growth of 9% in FY 2026 (was 4%) on higher sales growth and improved operating leverage," analyst Adrian Lemme says. Similarly, Citi sees discount department-store chain Kmart holding its own even as consumer spending picks up. It now forecasts 10% Ebit growth in Kmart, compared to 4% before. "While the valuation is high, we no longer see an obvious earnings catalyst to warrant a sell rating considering our bullish view on consumer spending into FY 2026," Citi says. (david.winning@wsj.com)

1903 ET [Dow Jones]--Lifestyle Communities has largely eliminated concerns about its balance sheet, and gets a new bull in Citi as a result. Analyst Suraj Nebhani says investors had been concerned about its interest cover ratio covenant of more than 1.75x heading into this week's annual earnings result. Lifestyle Communities said debt peaked at A$490 million in May, and is now trending down. It expects to shave around A$100 million more off its debt over the six months through December. "We believe that with the debt reductions, Lifestyle Communities may be able to be comfortable on covenants even with annual settlements at 200," Citi says. It upgrades Lifestyle Communities to buy, from neutral. "We also remove high risk rating given we no longer see covenant/capital raising risk," Citi says. (david.winning@wsj.com)

1848 ET - Jefferies is a big fan of everything about car dealership Eargers Automotive--except its valuation. Eagers's share price rose 12% on Thursday after beating expectations in 1H. Revenue of A$6.5 billion was 7% ahead of consensus hopes, while gross profit was 2% ahead of estimates. Jefferies says Eagers controls 14% of new vehicle sales in Australia, compared to the largest U.S. dealer, which has 2% of the market there. It's also big in used-vehicle sales. That makes it attractive for auto makers looking to establish a retail presence in Australia. Still, Jefferies says Eagers's valuation metrics following Thursday's short squeeze just look too high. "The market appears to be pricing in deals yet to happen," says analyst John Campbell. "We can't get there." (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1837 ET [Dow Jones]--Private hospital operator Ramsay Health Care expects earnings in Australia to rise in FY 2026. Jefferies thinks any improvement will be modest due to labor cost pressures. "Ramsay seen significant wage rises from all public payers, which likely has flow-on effects for the private sector," analyst David Stanton says. Wage bargaining negotiations have been finalized in New South Wales and Western Australia states, which represent 27% of group Ebit. Deals remain outstanding in Queensland and Victoria, also representing 27% of Ebit. Also, a new contract for Joondalup, Western Australia, will deal a A$37 million hit to Ebit. Jefferies retains a hold call on Ramsay, cuts its price target by 11% to A$34.50/share. Ramsay ended Thursday at A$34.09. (david.winning@wsj.com)

1754 ET - Caterpillar raises its expected cost for tariffs this year after hundreds of imported finished goods containing steel and aluminum were made eligible for 50% U.S. duties on the metal in them earlier this month. The construction equipment maker now expects to pay $1.5 billion to $1.8 billion in 2025, up from $1.3 billion to $1.5 billion expected earlier. The company says its 3Q tariffs expenses will be about $500 million to $600 million. The revised tariff outlook is not expected to affect the Caterpillar's sales forecast. Shares down 3.6% in after-hours trading. (robert.tita@wsj.com)

1625 ET - The Public Company Accounting Oversight Board postpones implementation of tighter rules on auditors' quality controls following pushback from audit firms. The rule will take effect on Dec. 15, 2026 instead of Dec. 15 this year, the audit watchdog says. The PCAOB adopted the rule in May 2024, followed by SEC approval. Firms will have to assess the risks associated with achieving effective quality controls and design procedures intended to address those risks. The Center of Audit Quality, which represents firms that audit public companies, asked to delay the implementation to allow firms more time to prepare. The SEC, which oversees the PCAOB, is seeking candidates for all five board seats, including the chair. Erica Williams resigned as PCAOB chair last month at SEC Chair Paul Atkins' request.(mark.maurer@wsj.com; @markgmaurer)

1605 ET - U.S. stocks end higher following a stronger-than-expected gains in 2Q GDP and a decline in weekly jobless claims. Federal Reserve governor Lisa Cook sues President Trump, seeking to block his move to fire her. Nvidia slips 0.8% after posting quarterly results Wednesday. Dick's Sporting Goods shares fall 4.8% despite strong earnings and raises full-year guidance. Communication services and technology sectors drive the gains, while utility stocks fall the most. DJIA rises71 points, or 0.2%, to 45636, and the S&P 500 gains 0.3% to 6501, both new records, while the Nasdaq climbs 0.5% to 21705. (patrick.sullivan@wsj.com)

data.title: Bapcor Remains a Work in Progress After Poor Result — Market Talk
data.description: Car parts company Bapcor's annual result does little to change Jefferies's view that it is a turnaround story. The result was poor overall, Jefferies says. Ebitda from its Trade segment in FY 2025 was up 5% on a year ago, but missed expectations by 3%. Retail earnings were also weaker than hoped. Analyst John Campbell says Bapcor's outlook commentary is very vague and suggests any material improvement is unlikely to be forthcoming in FY 2026. At best, it would seem any meaningful upturn from Bapcor isn't likely before 2H of FY 2027 at the earliest, Jefferies says. Bapcor is down 1.3% at A$3.89 in early trading. data.title: JAPAN JULY INDUSTRIAL OUTPUT: 9 OUT OF 15 INDUSTRIES POST FALLS, 6 ARE UP
Japan July factory output falls more than expected, retail sales disappoint
data.description:
Japan's factory output fell 1.6% in July from the previous month, worse than a median market forecast for a 1.0% fall, government data showed on Friday.

Manufacturers surveyed by the Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry expect seasonally adjusted output to grow 2.8% in August and dip 0.3% in September.

Separate data showed Japanese retail sales rose 0.3% in July from a year earlier, less than the median market forecast for a 1.8% rise.

data.title: JAPAN JULY RETAIL SALES -1.6% M/M (JUNE REVISED DOWN TO +0.9% FROM+1.0%); MEDIAN FORECAST +0.1% (RANGE: -0.6% TO +0.5%)
JAPAN JULY RETAIL SALES +0.3% Y/Y (JUNE REVISED DOWN TO +1.9 FROM +2.0%); 40TH STRAIGHT RISE; MEDIAN FORECAST +1.8% (RANGE: +1.0% TO +2.6%)
Gold Flat; Fed September Rate Decision Key to Near-Term Prices — Market Talk
data.description: Gold prices are flat in early Asian trade. Geopolitical tensions, renewed trade frictions and dented global sentiment have kept investors leaning on the yellow metal as a safe haven, Phillip Nova analyst Priyanka Sachdeva says in a research note. While profit-taking could cap gains for gold prices after the recent rally, broader macro drivers are still supportive, she notes. Gold prices will be shaped by incoming U.S. data such as the July PCE print, the analyst says, as they could cement expectations of a Fed rate cut in September. Spot gold is flat at $3,415.35/ oz. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: Emerald Green Trust Sells 90,676 Shares of Xos Common Stock
data.description:
Reporter NameEmerald Green Trust
Relationship10% Owner
TypeSell
Amount$274,076
SEC FilingForm 4

Emerald Green Trust, a 10% owner of Xos, reported the sale of 90,676 shares of common stock over three transactions on August 26, 27, and 28, 2025, with sales totaling $274,076. The weighted average selling prices were $3.0235, $3.0374, and $3.0001 respectively, indicating sales at varying prices. Following these transactions, Emerald Green Trust directly owns 1,700,854 shares of Xos common stock.

SEC Filing: Xos, Inc. [ XOS ] - Form 4 - Aug. 28, 2025

Frontier Lithium Reports First Quarter Results and Recent Highlights
data.description:
SUDBURY, ON, Aug. 28, 2025 /CNW/ - Frontier Lithium Inc. (FRA: HL2) ("Frontier Lithium" or the "Company") has reported financial results for the interim period ended June 30, 2025. The financial statements and the related management's discussion and analysis have been filed on SEDAR+ and can be viewed under the Company's profile at www.sedarplus.ca, as well as on the Company's website at www.frontierlithium.com.

Commenting on recent developments, President and Chief Executive Officer Trevor Walker stated:

"This quarter marks a number of important milestones for Frontier Lithium. We filed the Mine and Mill Feasibility Study, confirming the potential for a long-life operation producing an estimated 200,000 tonnes of spodumene concentrate annually, supported by highly competitive operating costs. Environmental baseline studies continue to advance, and Fluor Canada Ltd. has been appointed to lead the feasibility study for our lithium conversion facility, which is expected to be delivered in the first half of 2027.

Frontier Lithium was also proud to join Canada's mission to Germany, where a new cooperation on critical minerals was announced. Our PAK Lithium Project in northern Ontario hosts North America's highest-grade known hard-rock lithium resources, with the potential to support a fully integrated mine, mill, and conversion facility. With strong government support and aligned with the perspectives of our Indigenous partners, we are working to deliver a secure and responsibly sourced lithium supply for North America's electric vehicle and battery markets. Representing Frontier Lithium alongside Minister Hodgson in Berlin reflects both the progress our team has made in Ontario and Canada's broader commitment to sustainable battery materials for the global energy transition. This trade mission is a strong reminder of why we remain focused on our vision, and we are grateful to our partners, communities, and supporters who make milestones like this possible."

As of June 30, 2025, the Company had cash and cash equivalents of $15 million, providing a strong position to cover corporate and administrative expenses and advance its planned business objectives.

About Frontier Lithium

Frontier Lithium Inc. is a pre-production mining company with an objective to become a strategic and integrated supplier of premium spodumene concentrates as well as battery-grade lithium salts to the growing electric vehicle, and energy storage markets in North America. The Company's PAK Lithium Project maintains the largest land position and resource in a premium lithium mineral district located in Ontario's Great Lakes region.

About the PAK Lithium Project

The PAK Lithium Project is a fully integrated critical minerals initiative in Ontario, developing North America's highest-grade known lithium resource. Operated as a joint venture between Frontier (92.5%) and Mitsubishi Corporation (7.5%), the project is advancing in parallel with a mine and mill and a downstream conversion facility in Thunder Bay, Ontario both key to supporting a secure domestic lithium supply for the clean energy transition. A 2025 Mine and Mill Feasibility Study (FS), prepared by DRA and entitled National Instrument 43-101 Technical Report FS PAK Lithium Project, Mine and Mill, outlines a 31-year Project life with an after-tax net present value of CA $932 million at an 8% discount rate and an after-tax internal rate of return of 17.9%.These results were disclosed in a press release dated May 28, 2025, and the accompanying technical report was filed on July 9, 2025 on SEDAR+ (www.sedarplus.ca).

Cautionary Note Regarding Forward-Looking Statements

This news release includes certain statements that may be deemed "forward-looking statements". All statements in this release, other than statements of historical fact constitute forward-looking statements. Forward looking statements contained in this news release may include, but are not limited to, statements with respect to: capital requirements and private and/or public financing initiatives, estimated mineral resources, estimated capital costs to construct mine facilities, estimated operating costs, the duration of payback periods, estimated amounts of future production, estimated cash flows, net present value, the FS and statements that address future production, resource and reserve potential, exploration drilling, exploitation activities and events or developments that the Company expects, including but not limited to capital and operating costs, timelines, internal rates of return, and project development milestones.

Forward-looking statements involve inherent risks and uncertainties. Risk factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from those in forward looking statements include: market prices for commodities, increases in capital or operating costs, construction risks, availability of infrastructure including roads, regulatory and permitting risks, exploitation and exploration successes, continued availability of capital and financing, financing costs, and general economic, market or business conditions. Investors are cautioned that any such statements are not guarantees of future performance and those actual results or developments may differ materially from those projected in the forward-looking statements. For more information on the Company, please review the Company's public filings available at www.sedarplus.ca.

Neither the TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

SOURCE Frontier Lithium Inc.

View original content to download multimedia: http://www.newswire.ca/en/releases/archive/August2025/28/c4064.html

data.title: Autodesk Inc reports results for the quarter ended July 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Autodesk Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of $2.62​​ per share for the quarter ended July 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $2.15. The mean expectation of twenty four analysts for the quarter was for earnings of $2.45 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from $2.35 to $2.48 per share.

  • Revenue rose 17.1% to $1.76 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $1.72 billion.

  • Autodesk Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was $1.46​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $313 million.

  • Autodesk Inc shares had fallen by 7.6% this quarter and lost 3.3% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 24 "strong buy" or "buy," 8 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the software peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Autodesk Inc is $350.00, about 17.6% above its last closing price of $288.49

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data August 29 at 12:00 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Marvell Technology Inc reports results for the quarter ended July 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Marvell Technology Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 67 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended July 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 30 cents. The mean expectation of thirty one analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 66 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 27 cents to 69 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 57.6% to $2.01 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $2.01 billion.

  • Marvell Technology Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 22 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $194.8 million.

  • Marvell Technology Inc shares had fallen by 3.4% this quarter and lost 32.3% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 0.2% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 33 "strong buy" or "buy," 7 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the semiconductors peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Marvell Technology Inc is $90.00, about 14.2% above its last closing price of $77.23
